# SovereignSec-AI — Blackwell-critical version constraints.
#
# These guard the toolchain against accidental up/downgrades. They are a STARTING
# POINT — re-confirm exact versions against Unsloth's current Blackwell install doc
# on kickoff day (PLAN.md §2.4, §13). The real guarantee is ft-rig/env/verify_env.py,
# which inspects the *actual* runtime (cu128 build, sm_120, driver CUDA != 13.2).
#
# Hard invariants for RTX 5090 / sm_120 (these do NOT change):
#   - torch must be built for CUDA 12.8 (a "cu128" wheel)        → verify_env checks torch.version.cuda
#   - triton >= 3.3.1                                            (Blackwell support)
#   - bitsandbytes with cu128 wheels                             (4-bit QLoRA)
#   - CUDA toolkit 12.8                                          (NEVER 13.2 — gibberish on Qwen3.6)
#
# Versions below are indicative minimums known to support Blackwell; bump to the
# current Unsloth-recommended set on kickoff day, then freeze for the project.
torch>=2.7
triton>=3.3.1
bitsandbytes>=0.45.0
transformers>=4.48
datasets>=3.0
peft>=0.14
trl>=0.13
accelerate>=1.2
# unsloth + vllm are provided by the base image; pin them here too once you
# bare-metal install (uncomment and set the confirmed versions):
# unsloth==<kickoff-day-version>
# vllm==<kickoff-day-version>
